name: ECDLP attack-applicability registry — secp256k1
purpose: A machine-readable map of known ECDLP attack families and exactly where each known no-go ends and an unexplored zone begins. Consolidates the verified Lean substrate + honest experiments; invents no new attack. secp256k1 is NOT broken; prime-field ECDLP hardness is an open conjecture.
disclosure: Public — every entry is a defense/resistance/no-go or a documented open zone; no new attack technique. Any concrete practical speedup would move to a closed responsible-disclosure contour.

attacks.family: Generic-group model (Shoup / Nechaev black-box Omega(sqrt n) lower bound)
attacks.one_line: Machine-checked LOWER BOUND / no-go: any classical black-box DLP algorithm on a prime-order group of order n must form >= sqrt(n) group elements. For n ~ 2^256 the bound exceeds 2^127. It is a defense, not an attack; classical-generic only (false against Shor).
required_properties: Group order n is a large prime (ZMod n a field; affine-form difference has <=1 root)
n large: 2^255 < n so bound exceeds 2^127
MODEL ASSUMPTION (scope, not a curve property): algorithm is generic/black-box, never inspects (x,y) encoding

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Lower bound Omega(sqrt n) group operations for any generic classical solver; matched from above by BSGS/rho (GEN-BSGS-RHO-002) => classical generic ECDLP on secp256k1 is Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128. Only the arithmetic relation p <= q*q is formalized; no cost/step semantics.
attacks.memory: Not modeled; the formalized core is a memoryless information-theoretic collision count.
experimental_evidence: None. Machine-checked lower bound, no experiment; no experiment claims to beat sqrt(n) generically.
attacks.kill_criterion: Any black-box algorithm seeking sub-sqrt(n) work is PROVEN impossible against a prime-order group: generic_dlog_query_bound gives p <= q*q; secp256k1_generic_security instantiates 2^127 < q. Unconditional within the generic model.
open_escape_routes: CLASSICAL-GENERIC ONLY: a NON-generic algorithm reading the (x,y) encoding is outside the model. Non-existence of a fast prime-field ECDLP algorithm is UNPROVEN = the open hardness conjecture (BARRIERS.md, notes/SECURITY_SCOPE.md).
QUANTUM: false against Shor.
FORMALIZATION SCOPE: only the fixed-transcript affine-collision core is proved; the full adaptive Shoup/Nechaev oracle-query theorem is NOT formalized (GenericGroupBound.lean docstring).
attacks.verdict: No-go PROVED but SCOPE-LIMITED: best any classical generic algorithm does on secp256k1 is Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128 (machine-checked). Genuine formalization contribution, NOT unconditional hardness, false against quantum. Does not break secp256k1.
attacks.blocked_on: cost_model (BARRIERS.md B1: no group-operation/oracle-query framework in Mathlib) for the full adaptive theorem; beyond that, the open prime-field ECDLP hardness conjecture.
attacks.disclosure: LOWER bound (defense), not an attack. Classical-generic (black-box) only; prime-field ECDLP hardness remains an OPEN CONJECTURE.

attacks.family: Baby-step giant-step + Pollard rho / kangaroo (generic square-root collision search)
attacks.one_line: The concrete O(sqrt n) generic square-root algorithms. They DO solve secp256k1 ECDLP, but only in ~2^128 operations, matching the generic lower bound (GEN-GENERIC-001) — no shortcut. secp256k1's ~256-bit n is the entire defense.
required_properties: A finite cyclic group of known order n (always true for <G>); universal, needs no special structure
n large enough that sqrt(n) work is infeasible: for secp256k1 sqrt(n) ~ 2^128
collision-solve recovers x uniquely when (d-b) is a unit mod n (guaranteed n prime, d != b)

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: O(sqrt n) group ops: BSGS deterministic; rho ~sqrt(pi n/2). For secp256k1 ~2^128, matching Omega(sqrt n) => Theta(sqrt n), tight. No secp256k1-specific structure reduces this. bsgs_decomp / pollard_rho_collision are arithmetic covering+pigeonhole cores, not a formal running-time theorem.
attacks.memory: BSGS O(sqrt n) ~2^128 (infeasible). Pollard rho O(1) with distinguished points, fully parallelizable (time-area product stays sqrt n). rho, not BSGS, is the real generic threat — still Theta(sqrt n) time.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: sqrt(n) ~ 2^128; ceil(sqrt n) <= 2^128+1 (machine-checked). Both infeasible; no constant lowers the exponent.
experimental_evidence: experiments/p0_glv_semaev/RESULTS.md: EC pair-sum collision yield obeys yield ~ c*B^2/p (birthday / sqrt-law), B-exponent 2, p-exponent -1, on 16/20/24-bit toy curves (seed=1), each equality re-verified by ec_add. Consistent with Theta(sqrt n); no sub-sqrt behavior. Measures birthday occupancy, not a formal rho running time.
attacks.kill_criterion: NOT killed by secp256k1 structure — always applies. Neutralized purely by MAGNITUDE: Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128 (secp256k1_bsgs_steps_le pins ceil(sqrt n) <= 2^128+1). No verified fact reduces the exponent; the matching lower bound (GEN-GENERIC-001) shows none can generically.
open_escape_routes: Distinguished-point parallel rho (van Oorschot-Wiener): linear speedup, time-area product stays Theta(sqrt n). Not formalized (cost_model).
GLV/CM endomorphism constant ~sqrt(3) speedup — separate object GEN-GLV-003.
FORMALIZATION SCOPE: no cost/step model; running time and parallel speedups not stated as theorems, only arithmetic cores.
attacks.verdict: APPLICABLE but computationally INFEASIBLE. Best known classical attacks on prime-field ECDLP; solve secp256k1 in Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128, the generic optimum, no secp256k1-specific weakness. Secure by size of n, not by resistance. Does not break secp256k1.
attacks.blocked_on: cost_model (frontier_map.json / BARRIERS.md B1): blocks faithful sqrt(n) running-time statement and the parallel speedups.
attacks.disclosure: Succeed on any curve; secp256k1's defense is the 128-bit security level, not immunity. No shortcut is known or formalized.

attacks.family: GLV / CM endomorphism speedup (Pollard-rho automorphism ~sqrt(3); GLV scalar decomposition)
attacks.one_line: secp256k1 has j=0 and CM by Z[zeta_3]: the order-3 GLV endomorphism [lambda] permutes the group in orbits of size <=3, giving a CONSTANT ~sqrt(3) rho speedup (and ~2x scalar mult). Machine-checked SCOPED no-go: exponent-preserving, constant factor only.
required_properties: A nontrivial low-order efficiently computable endomorphism (CM). secp256k1 HAS it (j=0, CM by Z[zeta_3], order-3 phi(x,y)=(beta x,y))
rho speedup: automorphism induces orbits, shrinking search by orbit size
honest no-go: endomorphism applied to a DLP instance acts with the SAME scalar on base and target

secp256k1_status.property: PRESERVES the DLP exponent (self-reduction on the secret m)
secp256k1_status.evidence: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glv_preserves_dlog (Ecdlp/Proved/GlvNoGo.lean:49): Q=m.P => glvHom Q = m.glvHom P; secp256k1_glv_single_scalar (GlvNoGo.lean:59): one scalar k, k^2+k+1=0, acts identically on base and target

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Theta(sqrt n) STILL. Automorphism gives ~sqrt(3) fewer rho steps; with negation ~sqrt(6). GLV split k = k1 + k2*lambda gives ~2x scalar-mult (speedup for HONEST users). No exponent change.
attacks.memory: Same as Pollard rho (O(1) with distinguished points); care with fruitless cycles in the orbit quotient, no memory-order change.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: Orbit size 3; lambda order 3; beta order 3; rho ~sqrt(3)~1.73x, with negation ~sqrt(6)~2.45x; GLV half-length scalars ~2^128. Constants NOT machine-checked (no cost model).
experimental_evidence: experiments/p0_glv_semaev/RESULTS.md: GLV-orbit factor-base closure gives B_eff=3*store_B and only CONSTANT ~3x storage (c~5.45 for u=x^3, ~2.77x); B-exponent 2, p-exponent -1 — NO exponent change.
experiments/p1_petit + p1_petit_m3: solving S3=0 / S4=0 reproduces brute-force EC relation set EXACTLY (spurious=0); GLV closure constant ~3x only.
experiments/p3_sm_system: u=x^3 lowers d_reg NUMBER but enlarges Macaulay matrices ~15-20x, time ~80x on SAME variety — no advantage (capped pure-Python proxy).
experiments/p4_petit: composed map lowers d_reg number (7<9) but ~100x larger matrix, ~4300x slower — no net advantage; NOT faithful Petit.
attacks.kill_criterion: Endomorphism has order dividing 3 (glvPoint_cube_eq_id); only permutes in orbits <=3. Mapping (P, Q=m.P) yields (phi P, m.phi P) with SAME m (secp256k1_glv_preserves_dlog); one scalar k on base and target (secp256k1_glv_single_scalar). No new unknown, no new equation on m => exponent-preserving, buys at most the orbit constant.
open_escape_routes: SCOPED no-go, NOT universal: GlvNoGo proves only THIS map used THIS way preserves the exponent. 'CM-by-Z[zeta_3] gives no asymptotic advantage' is BELIEVED, not a theorem (GlvNoGo.lean docstring).
~sqrt(3)/2 rho and ~2x scalar-mult constants NOT formalized (no cost model).

HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001 is PARKED (experiments/HYPOTHESES.yaml): whether GLV-invariant RELATION GENERATION changes prime-field index-calculus asymptotics is mathematically OPEN, but no execution is authorized. Honest prior: LOW; expected negative. See IC-3.
attacks.verdict: CONSTANT-FACTOR speedup ONLY, exponent-preserving (machine-checked scoped no-go). Helpful for implementations, negligible for cryptanalysis. Asymptotic no-advantage of CM is believed but NOT proved. Does not break secp256k1.
attacks.blocked_on: cost_model (for the sqrt(3) constant) + the OPEN conjecture that CM gives no asymptotic advantage. HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001 is parked pending explicit route selection.
attacks.disclosure: Real CM structure; no-go is only SCOPED. Project's best coherent cryptanalytic bet, honest expected outcome NEGATIVE. Equality-replay alone cannot establish a complexity no-go.

attacks.family: Pohlig-Hellman (prime-factor decomposition of the group order)
attacks.one_line: PH reduces a DLP of order n to DLPs in prime-power subgroups; it NEEDS n composite/smooth. secp256k1's n is prime AND #E=n (cofactor 1), so the whole group has prime order — no proper subgroup to project into. DEFINITIVELY KILLED, unconditionally.
required_properties: n COMPOSITE/smooth: a proper nontrivial divisor d (1<d<n) so g^(n/d) generates an order-d subgroup
ideally n smooth so each per-subgroup DLP is easy
secp256k1 must FAIL these — and it does

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: PH = O(sum e_i(log n + sqrt p_i)). For n PRIME the only factor is n, so PH = Theta(sqrt n) = plain BSGS/rho — ZERO gain.
attacks.memory: Degenerates to a single order-n DLP = GEN-BSGS-RHO-002 profiles.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: n prime; largest (only) prime factor = n ~ 2^256, sqrt ~ 2^128. #E=n. (Aside: smooth part of n-1 is tiny, relevant to Cheon/p-1 on a DIFFERENT modulus, not PH on n.)
experimental_evidence: None needed — n prime and #E=n are machine-checked (Pratt + curve-cardinality certificate). Numeric leaves re-checked in scripts/certs/cardinality_secp256k1.py (prints CERT_OK).
attacks.kill_criterion: n is PRIME and #E=n (cofactor 1): no proper nontrivial factor d. Projection has no valid target; prime order => no proper subgroup. PH degenerates to a single order-n DLP = plain Theta(sqrt n). KILLED unconditionally.
open_escape_routes: NONE for classical PH while n stays prime — unconditional arithmetic fact. The historical loophole 'is published n the true group order?' is closed by secp256k1_card_point_eq_n.
ADJACENT (separate objects, NOT PH): Cheon p-1/p+1 target a different modulus; secp256k1's n-1 smooth part is tiny. See multi-target-precomputation.
attacks.verdict: DEFINITIVELY KILLED, UNCONDITIONALLY. Cleanest no-go in the map: an arithmetic fact, not a conjecture. Structurally cannot help against secp256k1.
attacks.blocked_on: Nothing. Fully resolved (n prime + #E=n both proved in the built tree).
attacks.disclosure: Inapplicable by design (prime n, cofactor 1). A resistance fact; asserts no attack.

attacks.family: Pairing transfer: MOV / Frey-Ruck (Weil) + Tate-Lichtenbaum (DEDUP of TRANSFER-MOV-FR and TRANSFER-TATE)
attacks.one_line: Weil (MOV/Frey-Ruck) and Tate pairings both embed the order-n subgroup into F_{p^k}^x (k = embedding degree, least k with n | p^k-1); if k is small, subexponential index calculus in F_{p^k} solves ECDLP. Both are governed by the SAME single embedding-degree obstruction, machine-checked absent for k<=100.
required_properties: small embedding degree k with n | p^k - 1 (pairing-agnostic: identical for Weil/MOV and Tate)
not supersingular is not a standalone defense; supersingular (k<=6) is one way to get small k
a computable Weil OR Tate pairing E[n]xE[n]->mu_n (the MECHANISM)
an n-torsion Q with pairing value of full order n (linear independence in E[n])

secp256k1_status.property: small embedding degree k

Mirror: Ecdlp.P256.p256_embedding_degree_gt_100 (P256EmbeddingDegree.lean). Defeats Weil and Tate identically (same target field).

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Transfer target is a DLP in F_{p^k}^x: L_{p^k}[1/3,~1.9] subexponential (NFS) WHEN k small. For secp256k1 k>100 => p^k > 2^25600, astronomically HARDER than direct generic Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128. Transfer is strictly counterproductive. Tate is cheaper to COMPUTE (one Miller loop + final exp) but shares the k-obstruction.
attacks.memory: Subexponential (NFS factor base) where applicable; N/A for secp256k1. Pairing evaluation itself O(log n) group ops (+ final exponentiation for Tate).
attacks.secp256k1_constants: p = 2^256-2^32-977 (secp256k1_p_prime); n prime, cofactor 1 (#E=n). Embedding degree machine-checked > 100; actual k externally known ~n, but only k>100 is kernel-certified.
experimental_evidence: None. Fully native_decide-verified embedding-degree fact; no experiment involved.
attacks.kill_criterion: Viable iff small k with p^k == 1 (mod n) (supersingular k<=6, MNT small-k). Machine-checked RULED OUT for 1<=k<=100. Verified frontier ENDS at k=100; k>100 not kernel-certified (only externally known large). Neither Weil nor Tate offers a smaller effective k for prime-field ordinary curves.
open_escape_routes: k in (100, infinity): only k<=100 machine-checked. A general lower bound (k ~ n) or extended native_decide range is an open formalization step (changes no security conclusion).

MECHANISM: Weil/Tate pairing e_n, Miller evaluation at a divisor, final exponentiation, and Weil reciprocity (W4) absent from Mathlib; would add NO new secp256k1 security fact beyond the embedding-degree bound.
attacks.verdict: RESISTED. Precondition (small k) machine-checked absent for k<=100; secp256k1 ordinary (t!=0). The pairing MECHANISM cannot even be stated (blocked-foundation). Resistance/formalization map, NOT a break.
attacks.blocked_on: MECHANISM: Weil/Tate pairing + Weil reciprocity (W4) absent from Mathlib (rungs W1-W3 + partial eval API built). BOUNDARY: native_decide certifies only k<=100.
attacks.disclosure: No security impact. Records secp256k1's (and P-256's) verified resistance to MOV/Frey-Ruck/Tate and the exact point (k>100, missing pairing) where formalization ends.

attacks.one_line: Over a non-prime extension F_{q^n} (n>1), Weil restriction builds a higher-genus cover over F_q whose Jacobian admits index calculus. secp256k1 is over a PRIME field F_p with no proper subfield, so there is nothing to descend to — resisted by construction, but with the WEAKEST kernel backing of the transfer families.
required_properties: base field a proper extension F_{q^n}, n>1, so a subfield F_q exists (Weil restriction nontrivial only for a proper extension)
a suitable GHS cover / hyperelliptic curve over F_q with the ECDLP faithfully embedded
genus/factor base keeping index calculus subexponential

secp256k1_status.property: base field is an extension F_{q^n}, n>1
secp256k1_status.evidence: secp256k1 is over the PRIME field F_p (Ecdlp.Primality.secp256k1_p_prime, Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean, Pratt). A prime field has no proper subfield => Weil restriction trivial. HONESTY: there is NO dedicated Lean no-go 'F_p prime => no Weil descent'; the inference is documented (BARRIERS.md B3, PUBLISHABLE_UNITS.md) but NOT machine-checked. The only in-source mentions are docstrings in SemaevDegree.lean and PointDecomposition.lean, not theorems.

secp256k1_status.evidence: No Weil restriction, cover/GHS construction, or genus-g Jacobian index calculus in Mathlib or the repo (notes/UPSTREAM_SCAN.md, notes/FOUNDATIONS.md)

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Where applicable (e.g. F_{2^n}, smooth composite n) GHS/Gaudry-Diem yields subexponential-to-polynomial DLP on the cover Jacobian. N/A for prime-field secp256k1: [F_p:F_p]=1, no descent target.
attacks.memory: Subexponential (genus-g cover factor base) where applicable; N/A for secp256k1.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: p prime (256-bit); F_p = ZMod p; [F_p:F_p]=1; only subfield is F_p. No composite extension degree.
lean_evidence: Ecdlp.Primality.secp256k1_p_prime (Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean) — establishes the PRIME base field; does NOT explicitly state 'no Weil descent'.
(No dedicated Weil-descent/GHS no-go theorem exists in the repo — confirmed by grep; resistance rests on prime-field structure + a documented, non-machine-checked argument.)
experimental_evidence: experiments/p4_petit/ (RESULTS.md, HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001): over a prime field there is 'no Weil descent / F_p-linear-subspace factor base to imitate', so P4 is a COMPOSED-map APPROXIMATION, not faithful GHS/Petit; measured no net advantage (d_reg number 7<9 but ~100x larger matrix, ~4300x slower). Honestly NOT a faithful Weil-descent attack.
attacks.kill_criterion: Targets composite-degree extension fields (F_{2^n} smooth n; F_{q^n}, n>1). secp256k1 over prime F_p is structurally immune, but the immunity is asserted from secp256k1_p_prime + a structural argument, NOT a dedicated Lean no-go. It would 'fail' only if the base field were non-prime, which secp256k1_p_prime forecloses.
open_escape_routes: FORMAL GAP: 'F_p prime => no proper subfield => Weil restriction trivial => GHS inapplicable' is NOT machine-checked; a formal no-go is an unbuilt, tractable-ish target — the honest place where this resistance rests on docs, not the kernel.
MECHANISM GAP: Weil restriction / cover theory entirely absent from Mathlib.

open_escape_routes: formalize a group-operation/oracle cost model in Mathlib (B1) so the batch/preprocessing exponent (S*T^2 ~ n) and Cheon bounds can be stated faithfully
the generic preprocessing lower bound (Corrigan-Gibbs-Kogan S*T^2 >~ n) is a candidate for the same information-theoretic cost-model sidestep that yielded the generic Omega(sqrt n) bound
measure the actual divisor structure of secp256k1 n±1 to close the Cheon-divisor 'unknown' row
attacks.verdict: NOT-APPLICABLE to single-target secp256k1 AND blocked-in-Lean: Cheon needs non-standard auxiliary input; batch/preprocessing never break a single 256-bit target; the exact trade-off cannot be stated without a cost model. Not a threat, not a break.
attacks.blocked_on: B1 cost_model (missing_from_mathlib) for the exact trade-off; plus the attack's own premise (auxiliary input / many same-group targets) is absent from the standard secp256k1 threat model.
attacks.disclosure: No security-relevant finding. Nothing here reduces single-target secp256k1 ECDLP below Theta(sqrt n).

attacks.family: side-channel / implementation: Hidden Number Problem -> lattice reduction (LLL/BKZ, CVP) / Bleichenbacher FFT
attacks.one_line: Partial leakage of ECDSA nonces k across many signatures forms a Hidden-Number-Problem lattice instance whose short/close vector reveals the private key. Orthogonal to ECDLP: it targets NONCE GENERATION, not the group; defeated by RFC 6979. secp256k1 hardness is untouched.
required_properties: many ECDSA signatures under one key with biased or partially-known nonces
a lattice-reduction oracle (LLL/BKZ) or CVP solver (or a Bleichenbacher FFT for sub-bit bias)
the ECDSA signing identity s*k = z + r*x, linear in x once k is known

secp256k1_status.property: first mile: bias -> HNP lattice -> nonce reconstruction
secp256k1_status.evidence: no lattice reduction (LLL/BKZ/CVP) in Mathlib (BARRIERS.md B2, ~24 claims); frontier bh-biased/bv-hnp/ns-ecdsa/minerva/ladderleak all blocked_on lattice_reduction

secp256k1_status.property: attack is an ECDLP / curve weakness
secp256k1_status.evidence: targets NONCE GENERATION, not the group; curve hardness Theta(sqrt n) is untouched; correct unbiased/deterministic nonce (RFC 6979) defeats it — implementation property, not curve property

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Polynomial in the number of signatures once enough bias is collected: ~2 known bits/signature => O(100s-1000s) signatures + one poly-time lattice reduction; <1 bit => Bleichenbacher/FFT over more samples. NOT expressible in Lean (no lattice or cost model).
attacks.memory: Lattice of dimension ~ number of HNP samples; BKZ block memory. Bleichenbacher FFT: large table over the sample range. Not formalized.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: Lives entirely in the scalar field ZMod n (256-bit prime order). Curve constants (a=0, b=7, beta, lambda) irrelevant — the attack never touches the group law.
attacks.kill_criterion: Deterministic, constant-time, unbiased nonce generation (RFC 6979) removes the HNP samples => the attack has no input. Implementation property; secp256k1 the curve neither causes nor prevents it, so no curve-level fact registers as defense or weakness.
open_escape_routes: port lattice reduction (LLL/BKZ) + CVP to Mathlib (B2) to formalize the HNP reduction — months-scale; prior art is Isabelle/HOL only (notes/UPSTREAM_SCAN.md)
formalize the Boneh-Venkatesan HNP-to-lattice reduction abstractly (statement/correctness)
formalize the Bleichenbacher FFT bias-exploitation bound (also blocked: needs a cost model)
attacks.verdict: Real-world attack but NOT a curve break: verified last-mile algebra, blocked first-mile (B2, no lattice reduction). Defeated by correct nonces; does NOT reduce secp256k1 ECDLP.
attacks.disclosure: Well-known implementation attack class (Sony PS3 2010, 2013 Android SecureRandom Bitcoin thefts, Minerva, LadderLeak). The repo formalizes only the public final algebra. Not a secp256k1 ECDLP weakness.

attacks.one_line: A fault-tolerant quantum computer solves ECDLP in polynomial time via Shor period-finding, breaking secp256k1 outright; the classical generic Theta(sqrt n) bound explicitly does not apply. A documented EXTERNAL fact, out of scope for this substrate (no quantum cost model in Mathlib).
required_properties: a cryptographically-relevant / fault-tolerant quantum computer of sufficient logical-qubit count and gate depth
reversible quantum circuits for elliptic-curve point addition over F_p
quantum Fourier transform / period finding

attacks.asymptotic_complexity: Quantum polynomial time: ~O((log n)^3) qubit-operations / poly(log n) depth. Not expressible in Lean (no quantum cost model).
attacks.memory: 835 logical qubits for secp256k1 in Luo et al. 2026 (arXiv:2607.13816); physical-qubit count and runtime depend on error correction, architecture, and depth. External logical-resource estimate, not a repo measurement.
attacks.secp256k1_constants: 256-bit order n; Shor cost scales with bit-length, not curve structure — GLV / j=0 / beta confer no quantum defense and no extra quantum weakness.
attacks.kill_criterion: N/A as a mathematical no-go — Shor is a proven poly-time quantum algorithm; no curve property defeats it. The only 'escape' is engineering: CRQCs at the needed scale do not yet exist. Migration to post-quantum cryptography is the mitigation.
open_escape_routes: out of scope for Mathlib — no planned path; would require a full quantum circuit cost model
the honest boundary is the scope note on the classical generic bound (notes/SECURITY_SCOPE.md); no formal quantum statement is attempted

attacks.disclosure: Publicly known since Shor 1994. No new finding. Recorded only as the standing classical-vs-quantum scope boundary of the generic bound. The substrate does NOT claim to break secp256k1; this object records an external limitation of classical security, not a repo result.

edges.statement: The contradiction between results stated on the base-point subgroup <G> versus the full curve group E(F_p) is resolved by the keystone #E(F_p)=n (cofactor 1, no Hasse/Schoof). Every <G>-scoped resistance fact now holds on all of E(F_p): PH kill extends to E(F_p); GLV eigenvalue scope is pinned; the Frobenius trace t=p+1-n is genuine so MOV/anomalous no-gos become unconditional (non-anomalousness follows from #E=n!=p, not a pinned order).
edges.status: landed (imported in Ecdlp.lean:103, VERIFIED.md row 237, PR #149)

edges.statement: Cluster 1's TRANSFER-MOV-FR (Weil/Frey-Ruck) and TRANSFER-TATE (Tate-Lichtenbaum) are deduplicated into one pairing-transfer object: both embed into the same F_{p^k}^x and are governed by the SAME single embedding-degree obstruction (secp256k1_embedding_degree_gt_100), and both share the identical foundation-rung gap (Weil reciprocity W4 / pairing value absent from Mathlib). Cluster 1 itself flagged Tate as foldable into MOV.

Cluster 1's TRANSFER-ANOMALOUS-SSSA and Cluster 2's IC-5-padic-lifting are deduplicated into one p-adic/lifting object: IC-5 already subsumes the anomalous Smart/SSSA route (same anchors secp256k1_trace_ordinary_nonanomalous + anomalous_iff_trace_one) and adds xedni calculus + general lifting. The merged object keeps the sharp anomalous no-go and the empirically-negative xedni/lifting routes, plus jacobson_xedni_dependence_recovers_log.

The generic Omega(sqrt n) lower bound (GEN-GENERIC-001) and the concrete O(sqrt n) BSGS/Pollard-rho upper bound (GEN-BSGS-RHO-002) are complementary, not contradictory: together they pin classical generic ECDLP on secp256k1 at Theta(sqrt n) ~ 2^128, tight. secp256k1's defense is the size of n, not a structural gap.

GEN-GLV-003 (rho automorphism + GLV scalar-mult speedup, generic model) and IC-3 (GLV-symmetrized Semaev factor base, index-calculus model) are kept distinct but share the same machine-checked CM facts (glvPoint_cube_eq_id, secp256k1_lambda_orderOf, secp256k1_glvPoint_eq_lam_on_zmultiples, secp256k1_beta_orderOf). Both conclude CONSTANT ~3x / ~sqrt(3) only, no exponent change; neither is a break. Not merged because they act on different attack surfaces (rho search vs relation generation).

IC-1 is the general prime-field index-calculus framework; IC-2 (Semaev summation engine) is its algebraic core, refined by IC-3 (GLV invariant coordinates) and IC-4 (Petit composed map). All four share the SAME structural barrier: no Weil restriction over a prime field (BARRIERS.md B3), so the single high-degree Semaev equation cannot be split. This shared barrier is where the descent no-go (TRANSFER-WEIL-DESCENT) ends and the open zone begins.

TRANSFER-WEIL-DESCENT's adjacent open zone IS the prime-field index-calculus question (IC-1/IC-2): whether some prime-field structure can substitute for the missing Weil restriction in a Semaev/Petit index calculus. HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001 is PARKED; P0-P4 are partial negatives (constant ~3x only). This edge marks precisely where a machine-checked-by-construction no-go ends and unexplored territory begins.

TRANSFER-WEIL-DESCENT's resistance rests on secp256k1_p_prime PLUS a documented structural argument (prime field has no proper subfield => Weil restriction trivial), NOT on a dedicated Lean no-go theorem. This is the WEAKEST kernel backing among the transfer families and is recorded in flags.

The exact running-time / trade-off exponents for these families cannot be stated in Lean because Mathlib lacks a group-operation/oracle cost model (B1), lattice reduction (B2, ~24 claims), and a quantum circuit cost model (B2, ~38 claims). The generic Omega(sqrt n) and MOV/Smart resistance facts were reached by sidestepping the cost model with an information-theoretic core; the trade-off/lattice/quantum families have no such sidestep and remain blocked-foundation.

The prime-order keystone secp256k1_n_prime (Pratt certificate) underwrites: the field structure for the generic collision bound, the unit condition for collision-recovery, the no-subgroup Pohlig-Hellman kill, and the cofactor-1 cardinality argument.

The prime-FIELD keystone secp256k1_p_prime (Pratt certificate) underwrites the prime-field structure: no proper subfield (no Weil restriction target), F_p base field for the embedding-degree computation, and the extension-degree-1 fact behind the index-calculus barrier.

cost_schema.purpose: The fields every ECDLP relation-generation hypothesis/experiment must carry so runs are comparable and no-go claims are auditable. Values are filled for the reproducible experiments P0-P4 where measured, and 'unmeasured' otherwise. HONESTY FLAGS: P3's degree_of_regularity for m>=3 is an UNVALIDATED pure-Python Macaulay-matrix proxy (capped lower bound only); P4 is NOT faithful Petit (a prime field affords no Weil restriction, so no F_p-linear-subspace base to imitate) - it is an explicitly-labelled composed-low-degree-map approximation.

fields.nvars: number of unknowns in the relation system solved per relation (1 = univariate solve; m = plain S_{m+1} system; 2m or 3m = invariant/composed-map coupled system).
fields.equation_degree: degree of the governing summation polynomial / defining equation (S3 = 2 per var; S4 <= 4; S_m = 2^(m-2); factor-base polynomial f_F = |F|).
fields.degree_of_regularity: solving degree of the Groebner/Macaulay computation - the cost-driving quantity the hypothesis targets; distinct from and larger than the reduced-GB output degree.
fields.factor_base_size: |F|, number of factor-base x-coordinates (B_eff = 3B under GLV orbit closure).
fields.relation_probability: probability a random attempt yields a valid relation (or yield law c*B_eff^2/p in the lookup model); spurious-root rate is the empirical signature of Semaev iff-theorems.
fields.linear_algebra_cost: size/cost of the Macaulay matrix or linear-algebra step (rows x cols, wall time).
fields.memory: peak storage: hash table (lookup model), factor-base storage, or Macaulay matrix footprint.
fields.parallelization: available parallel speedup (van Oorschot-Wiener distinguished points: Theta(m) shared, Theta(sqrt m) naive) - measured or unmeasured.
fields.quantum_or_classical: cost model regime; all P0-P4 are classical.
fields.toy_scaling: toy-curve bit sizes and |F| range actually run.
fields.projection_128bit: extrapolation to cryptographic (128-bit security / 256-bit n) parameters, if any - deliberately 'unmeasured / not projected' where only constant factors or descriptive fits exist.

P0_glv_semaev_m2_lookup.degree_of_regularity: unmeasured (lookup/enumeration model measures no degree of regularity)
P0_glv_semaev_m2_lookup.factor_base_size: B toy; B_eff = 3B under GLV orbit closure
P0_glv_semaev_m2_lookup.relation_probability: yield ~ c*B_eff^2/p; c ~ 5.45 for u=x^3 key vs plain (~2.8x), i.e. constant ~3x, NO exponent change
P0_glv_semaev_m2_lookup.linear_algebra_cost: N/A - direct EC-pair enumeration is Theta(B^2), not index calculus
P0_glv_semaev_m2_lookup.toy_scaling: 16/20/24-bit toy curves, seed=1; every equality re-verified by real ec_add

P1_petit_m2_S3_solve.degree_of_regularity: N/A (single univariate solve, not a system)
P1_petit_m2_S3_solve.relation_probability: spurious-root rate = 0 across 16/20/24-bit; relation set identical to brute-force EC (complete + sound)
P1_petit_m2_S3_solve.linear_algebra_cost: N/A - O(|F|) field solves per target replacing O(|F|^2) pair enumeration
P1_petit_m2_S3_solve.memory: GLV/u=x^3 gives constant ~3x storage reduction only

P1_petit_m3_S4_solve.equation_degree: S4 = Res_X(S3,S3) <= 4 in the unknown (unit-tested 4 ways)
P1_petit_m3_S4_solve.degree_of_regularity: N/A (per-pair univariate degree-4 solve, not a reduced system)
P1_petit_m3_S4_solve.relation_probability: spurious = 0 in all 12 settings; S4-solver relation set IDENTICAL to O(|F|^3) brute-force (600 targets, 105 relations, VALIDATION PASS)
P1_petit_m3_S4_solve.linear_algebra_cost: N/A - O(|F|^2 * deg4-solve); NOT a subexponential index-calculus algorithm

P3_sm_system_groebner.nvars: m (plain S_{m+1} system); 2m for the coupled u=x^3 invariant system {S_{m+1}=0, U_i-X_i^3=0, f_{F,u}(U_i)=0}
P3_sm_system_groebner.equation_degree: S_{m+1} + factor-base poly f_F degree |F|; invariant f_{F,u} degree |F|/3
P3_sm_system_groebner.degree_of_regularity: m=2 plain solving degree = 2|F|+1 EXACTLY (|F|=4,6,8,10,12), LINEAR in |F| and p-INDEPENDENT; u=x^3 ~2|F|/3+5 (lower NUMBER); m=3 (S4) only a CAPPED LOWER BOUND >15 - UNVALIDATED pure-Python Macaulay proxy, full solving degree needs msolve/Sage/F4
P3_sm_system_groebner.relation_probability: spurious = 0; independent brute-force EC cross-check IDENTICAL (100 targets, 40 relations, VALIDATION PASS)
P3_sm_system_groebner.linear_algebra_cost: Macaulay matrices grow ~|F|^2; u=x^3 system ~15-20x LARGER, ~80x SLOWER at matched |F| (same variety f_F(X)=f_{F,u}(X^3))
P3_sm_system_groebner.memory: Macaulay matrix dimensions measured; m=3 graded reduction intractable in pure Python
P3_sm_system_groebner.projection_128bit: descriptive-only fits, NO asymptotic/advantage claim, not projected

P4_petit_composed_map_m2.equation_degree: product_2aux per-equation degree <= max(2,b0) ~ sqrt(|F|); single_aux g(t) degree ~|F| (degree only relocated)
P4_petit_composed_map_m2.degree_of_regularity: product_2aux solving degree = 7 (BELOW raw 2|F|+1=9); single_aux = 9 capped (NOT below raw) - measured only at |F|=4, m=2; NOT faithful Petit (approximation, no Weil restriction over prime field)
P4_petit_composed_map_m2.relation_probability: 0.000 random (birthday regime); all constructed targets recovered, spurious=0; independent gate 80 targets/32 relations, composed==raw==brute-force EC, VALIDATION PASS
P4_petit_composed_map_m2.linear_algebra_cost: Macaulay 10758x5005 vs raw 108x78 (~100x rows, ~64x cols); 619s vs 0.14s (~4300x slower), SAME quotient dimension (2), SAME variety
P4_petit_composed_map_m2.projection_128bit: descriptive-only, no asymptotic/advantage/no-go-proof claim, not projected

cost_schema.cross_cutting_conclusion: At every rung the GLV / u=x^3 / composed-map orbit closure buys only a CONSTANT (~3x) storage reduction; the summation-polynomial solve reproduces the brute-force relation set with ZERO spurious roots (empirical signature of the Semaev iff-theorems S₃_eq_zero_iff / S₄_common_root_of_eq_zero); where reachable, degree_of_regularity is set by the factor-base DEFINING degree - neither invariant nor composed coordinates buy a net advantage (lower degree NUMBER is always paid back as a larger ring / larger Macaulay matrix / slower time on the SAME variety). No rung is a step toward breaking secp256k1; HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001 is mathematically open but execution is PARKED.

priority_function.formula: Priority(H) = P(H) * Impact(H) * InfoGain(H) / (ProofCost + ExperimentCost + SecurityRisk)

terms.P(H): Calibrated prior that H yields a genuine, explained result - either a real speedup OR a rigorous no-go; deliberately LOW for prime-field subgeneric routes (~0.05 for HYP_GLV_SEMAEV_001, per its honest_prior).
terms.Impact(H): Magnitude if resolved: a prime-field ECDLP exponent change = paradigm-shifting; a machine-checked barrier/no-go or scope boundary = publishable unit; a constant-factor speedup = negligible.
terms.InfoGain(H): Reduction in frontier uncertainty per run, INCLUDING negative results that CLOSE a tested window - a no-go scores high on InfoGain even when P(speedup) ~ 0.
terms.ProofCost: Lean/Mathlib effort to state and kernel-verify (weeks per rung; MONTHS for a missing foundation such as the Weil pairing, lattice reduction, or a cost model).
terms.ExperimentCost: Compute + engineering to run a reproducible benchmark with an INDEPENDENT validator (toy primes cheap; msolve/Sage/F4-scale or m>=4 systems expensive).
terms.SecurityRisk: Penalty for routes that could actually threaten secp256k1 before responsible disclosure; near-zero here by design (honest-scope, no-break), but any nonzero-break route trips a DISCLOSURE GATE (private handling) instead of a public race - it raises the denominator, it does not raise Priority.

priority_function.conventions: Higher Priority => work it sooner. Formalize in Lean ONLY on a positive, explained result or a clean barrier statement; a numerical confirmation of known structure triggers NO formalization (see HYP_WARD_EDS_001 exit_criteria). Prefer routes that sidestep a missing foundation via an information-theoretic core (how the generic Omega(sqrt n) bound and the MOV/Smart resistance facts were reached) over routes that require building the full missing foundation.

worked_examples.assessment: P high, Impact medium-high (publishable), InfoGain high, ProofCost low (docs+existing theorems), ExperimentCost ~0, SecurityRisk 0 => HIGH Priority - the strongest near-term output.

P ~0.05, Impact very high IF positive, InfoGain high (each rung closes a window), ExperimentCost rising steeply (m>=4 needs msolve/Sage), SecurityRisk low but nonzero-if-positive (disclosure gate) => MODERATE Priority as an experiment, LOW as an expected break; run through the substrate, not as a race.

P low for single-target secp256k1 (needs auxiliary input / many targets), Impact medium, InfoGain medium, ProofCost blocked on cost model (high) => LOW Priority until a cost model exists; the generic-preprocessing lower bound is the higher-ROI sub-target.

P n/a as a curve break (orthogonal), Impact medium (implementation), InfoGain medium, ProofCost blocked on lattice reduction (months, B2), SecurityRisk low (public class) => LOW Priority in Lean; last-mile algebra already verified.

Impact maximal but P(formalizable-here) = 0 (out of scope, no quantum cost model), InfoGain ~0 beyond the existing scope note => OUT OF SCOPE / lowest Priority; record only as the classical-vs-quantum boundary.

flags: NAMING (resolved, not a fabrication): the source clusters cite Semaev theorems in ASCII - `S3_eq_zero_iff`, `S4`, `S4_eq_zero_of_common_root`, `S4_common_root_of_eq_zero`, `secp256k1_S3poly_natDegree` - which do NOT literally appear. The real Lean theorems use Unicode subscripts and are verified: `S₃_eq_zero_iff` (Ecdlp/Proved/SemaevThree.lean:146), `S₄` (SemaevFour.lean:71), `S₄_eq_zero_of_common_root` (SemaevFour.lean:81), `S₄_common_root_of_eq_zero` (SemaevFour.lean:140), `secp256k1_S₃poly_natDegree` (SemaevDegree.lean:43). Normalized to Unicode in this bundle.

WEAKEST BACKING (self-flagged, carried): TRANSFER-WEIL-DESCENT has NO dedicated Lean no-go theorem. 'Prime field => no Weil descent' rests on secp256k1_p_prime (Ecdlp/Proved/Secp256k1PrimeP.lean) PLUS a documented structural argument (BARRIERS.md B3, PUBLISHABLE_UNITS.md); the only in-source mentions of Weil restriction are docstrings in SemaevDegree.lean and PointDecomposition.lean, not theorems. Confirmed by grep: no Weil-descent/GHS no-go theorem exists in the repo.

HONESTLY EMPTY: IC-4-petit-composed-map has an intentionally empty lean_evidence array - no Lean formalization of Petit or Weil descent exists in the repo (green-field upstream). This is honest, not a gap masked as a citation.

EXPERIMENTAL CAVEAT (not Lean): P3's degree_of_regularity for m>=3 is an UNVALIDATED pure-Python Macaulay-matrix proxy (a capped lower bound >15); the full solving degree needs an external engine (msolve/Sage/F4). No asymptotic conclusion is drawn from it.

EXPERIMENTAL CAVEAT (not Lean): P4 is NOT faithful Petit - a prime field affords no Weil restriction, so there is no F_p-linear-subspace factor base to imitate; P4 is an explicitly-labelled composed-low-degree-map approximation, measured only at |F|=4, m=2, toy primes.

SCOPE (not a fabrication): Ecdlp.GenericGroup.secp256k1_generic_security takes a [Fact (Nat.Prime Secp256k1.n)] instance (supplied by secp256k1_n_prime); the formalized core is the fixed-transcript affine-collision count, NOT the full adaptive Shoup/Nechaev oracle-query theorem (GenericGroupBound.lean docstring). Primality is a proved hypothesis, not an axiom.

LINE-NUMBER PRECISION: every cited theorem identifier was grep-confirmed to resolve to its named file; individual line numbers were spot-checked (e.g. SemaevFour.lean:71/81/140, GlvAutomorphism.lean:31, CurveCardinalityExact.lean:172) but not exhaustively re-verified for every citation. Identifiers are real; a few cited line numbers may drift by a small amount.

EXTERNAL (not a repo result): Luo et al. 2026 (arXiv:2607.13816) report a space-optimized secp256k1 circuit estimate of 835 logical qubits and 2^30.63 Toffoli gates. It supersedes their April 2026 preprint, remains an external logical-resource estimate, and does not establish available fault-tolerant hardware. Quantum resource claims are not machine-checked repo facts.

UNKNOWN (honest): the n±1 divisor structure a Cheon exponent would need is NOT measured in this substrate; only the tiny smooth part of p-1 = 2*3*7*13441 is documented (notes/PRIMALITY.md), which concerns F_p^x / p-1 routes, not the order-n curve subgroup. Marked unknown, not guessed.
